steps.title: Factor the denominator
steps.content:
Use the difference of squares.
$$
\pi^2-x^2=(\pi-x)(\pi+x)
$$
Since $x+\pi$ is the same as $\pi+x$, this can be the common denominator.

steps.title: Subtract the numerators
steps.content: Now subtract over the common denominator. $$ \frac{18\pi^2}{\pi^2-x^2}-\frac{18\pi(\pi-x)}{\pi^2-x^2}=\frac{18\pi^2-18\pi(\pi-x)}{\pi^2-x^2} $$ $$ \frac{18\pi^2-18\pi(\pi-x)}{\pi^2-x^2}=\frac{18\pi x}{\pi^2-x^2} $$ The answer is B.
